items.briefing:
Australia's cyber security agency (ASD) has issued a warning about "device code phishing" — a scam where criminals trick you into handing over login access by getting you to enter a code on a legitimate-looking page. This technique is increasingly being packaged into easy-to-use criminal toolkits, meaning more attackers can use it with less skill. Separately, GitHub has confirmed a breach affecting around 4,000 internal repositories, which may affect developers or teams using GitHub-hosted code.

Android users should also be cautious of fake apps quietly signing them up for paid services without consent. If you downloaded an unfamiliar app recently, check your phone bill for unexpected charges.

**Tip:** Be suspicious of any unexpected prompt asking you to enter a code on a website to "verify" your identity — contact your IT team before proceeding.

featured.title: ASD warns of device code phishing as Proofpoint tracks growing criminal toolkits
featured.quote: ASD warning on Microsoft 365 phishing directly impacts millions of Australian business users.
